title: hopea glabra
intro: Hopea glabra is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia, particularly in the rainforests of Malaysia and Indonesia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Hopea glabra include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide protection against various diseases. The herbal preparations of Hopea glabra include a decoction made from the bark and leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application to treat skin conditions.
